content: Clinical implementation examples: CYP2C19 poor metabolizers on clopidogrel have reduced active metabolite, higher cardiovascular event rates - use prasugrel or ticagrelor instead. CYP2D6 poor metabolizers: codeine/tramadol ineffective (need conversion to active metabolites); use morphine directly. CYP2D6 ultrarapid metabolizers: codeine toxicity risk (rapid conversion to morphine) - avoid entirely. TPMT deficiency: thiopurine (azathioprine, 6-MP) dose reduction 50-90% or alternative agent to prevent life-threatening myelosuppression. UGT1A1*28 homozygotes: irinotecan dose reduction 30% to prevent severe neutropenia/diarrhea. HLA-B*5701 screening mandatory before abacavir - positive = contraindicated. Warfarin: CYP2C9/VKORC1 genotyping guides initial dose but INR monitoring still required.
